issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast (section header paragraph)
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 3:1 for large text (18px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 825: .section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only outline-offset and border-radius, which do not create a visible focus indicator. Keyboard users will not see which element has focus when navigating with the Tab key.
issuesFound.fix: Line 375: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default focus outline with outline: none and only sets outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for button elements when navigated via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 399: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size (search submit button)
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a touch target well below the recommended 44px size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 745: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (checkbox input)
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the minimum 24px touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1502: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Blockquote text contrast failure
issuesFound.explanation: Blockquote text uses colour #aaaaaa on a white background #ffffff, resulting in a contrast ratio of 2.9:1 which fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 353: color: #767676;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, which renders no visible ring since the outline width is zero. This removes keyboard focus indication for all elements that do not override this rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 393: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify focused buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and auto dimensions, making content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only.
issuesFound.fix: Line 426: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: normal; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion not reduced
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses a transform transition that is not disabled in the empty prefers-reduced-motion media query. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 489: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px by 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 816: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size (button small variant)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36px with only 6px vertical padding, resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px size without sufficient padding compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size (footer navigation links)
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set a min-height of 24px without sufficient padding compensation, resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px size for optimal accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1549: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation lacks reduced motion override (button)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial movement for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: (add media query) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:active { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation lacks reduced motion override (hero CTA)
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition and applies transform: translateY(-1px) on hover. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 899: (add media query) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary, .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation lacks reduced motion override (skip link)
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class uses transition: transform var(--transition-base) which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: (add media query) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px as a replacement. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring; it merely offsets a zero-width outline. Keyboard users will see no focus indicator when navigating through interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px as replacement. This removes the visible focus ring for buttons, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify which button has focus. The outline-offset property requires a non-zero outline width to be visible.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto instead of the required position: absolute with 1px dimensions and clip properties. This fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers, causing visual layout issues while potentially exposing content that should be hidden.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause spatial movement animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform transition for users who have requested reduced motion, potentially triggering vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, ensuring focus indicators are invisible even in Windows High Contrast Mode where system colors are enforced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 226-228: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, removing the default focus ring without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 215: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which animates spatial movement, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 380: .btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); text-align: center; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); vertical-align: middle; position: relative; isolation: isolate; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }` removes the default focus ring without providing any visible replacement, leaving keyboard‑only users without a focus indicator on all interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px;` (below the 24 px minimum), resulting in a touch target that is too small for finger interaction.

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em;` which compresses spacing between words, reducing readability for all users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component suppresses the focus outline with `outline: none; outline-offset: 3px;` but does not add a visible alternative, so keyboard users cannot see which button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary` includes a `transform` transition (scale/translate) but there is no corresponding `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule to disable it, violating reduced‑motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` defines `min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px;` which is below the required 24 px minimum, creating an insufficient touch area.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-primary` inherits the `transform` transition from `.btn` but does not have a reduced‑motion override, leaving the animation active for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non‑colour cue
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no alternative visual cue (e.g., border‑bottom, font‑weight change) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on colour alone.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the focus outline (`outline: none; outline-offset: 3px;`) without any replacement, making it invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible replacement. This hides focus indicators in High Contrast Mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 343: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. outline-offset applies to a zero-width outline and renders nothing, removing the focus ring for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 333: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector sets outline-color on the base :focus-visible rule which has outline: none. Setting a color on a non-existent outline provides no visible focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 351: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link

issuesFound.fix: Line 433: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: Section header paragraphs use var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa (rgb(170,170,170)) on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.3:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (requires 4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 13: --colour-text-secondary: #757575; (or darker) or Line 421: .section-header p { color: var(--colour-neutral-700); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline width. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users entirely, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 295: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below the default. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and those using screen magnification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 292: Remove the word-spacing declaration or set to normal: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector only adjusts outline-offset to -3px but does not restore a visible outline width or style. Since the global :focus-visible rule (line 295) sets outline: none, this results in no visible focus indicator for elements using this class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 313: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a touch target of 28px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it falls below the 44px recommended size and lacks compensating padding to reach the recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 549: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. This removes the focus indicator specifically in Windows High Contrast Mode where it is most needed, leaving users without any visible focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 358: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users without any visible indication of focus. This affects all interactive elements in the document.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.2 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 497: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making it harder for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities to distinguish word boundaries and read text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 125: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The utility class .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible only sets outline-offset: -3px. Since the base :focus-visible rule sets outline: none, this results in no visible focus indicator for elements using this class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto. This fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible; instead, it renders content normally in the layout. Screen reader only text will be visible to all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 376: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle has min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px with padding: var(--space-2) (6px). The total touch target is 40px vertically (28px + 6px + 6px), below the recommended 44px. Insufficient padding fails to compensate for the small dimensions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 480: .nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 28px; min-width: 28px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: var(--space-3); background: none; border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-md); color: var(--colour-text-primary); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. This creates a 20×20px touch target that is too small for reliable activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 509: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu links and buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu items have min-height: 28px with padding: var(--space-2) (6px) vertically. This creates a 40px total height, below the recommended 44px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 506: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1); padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-3); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-md); border: none; background: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 28px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links without providing another visual cue (e.g., color contrast, border, or background), making links indistinguishable from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the default focus outline (`outline: none`) and only adds an offset, providing no visible focus indicator. Keyboard‑only users cannot see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: `word-spacing: -0.2em;` on the body creates overlapping words, reducing readability for all users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component suppresses the focus outline (`outline: none`) without supplying a visible alternative, making focus invisible for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `clip: auto`, which does **not** hide content from visual layout but also does **not** keep it accessible; it effectively removes it from assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1890: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the menu to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2296: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` and before the `addClass` calls.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() function displays the search suggestions panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element. Screen readers will not announce that the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;` and before the `if (!_inputEl.value)` check.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2430: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` and before `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions change the visible state of dropdown menus but do not write to an aria-live region to announce the state change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2430: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` before the emit call. Line 2440: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles visual active states on filter buttons but does not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to screen readers. These buttons act as toggle buttons and require aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3600: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands the FAQ answer panel using animateHeight but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4180: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` and before `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField missing aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: The _addFile() function validates file size and type but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the file input when validation fails, preventing screen readers from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5050: In the validation failure block after `liveRegion.announceAssertive(...)`, add `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.open()` makes the drawer visible but never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the `open()` function after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened or closed, no `aria-live` region is updated, so users of screen readers receive no audible feedback about the change.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the panel’s visibility, update a dedicated live region (e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `'Menu closed'`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger click handler does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: `_onTriggerClick` toggles the dropdown panel visibility but only calls `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing; it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` when opening.
issuesFound.fix: In the branch that opens the panel, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria‑pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) toggles the navigation drawer but never updates `aria-pressed`, which is required for a toggle button that represents an on/off state.
issuesFound.fix: In the `toggle()` or `open()`/`close()` logic, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')` when opening and `'false'` when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` expands the answer panel but never updates the question button’s `aria-expanded` attribute, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open()` function.

issuesFound.title: FAQ items do not initialise aria‑expanded to false on page load
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers are created without an explicit `aria-expanded="false"` when they start collapsed, leaving the default state ambiguous for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: During initialization, for each closed item, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. The markValid function removes aria-invalid, meaning fields are never marked as invalid programmatically, preventing screen readers from announcing the error state correctly.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the mobileNav module adds the open class and announces the state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module updates the UI and emits an event but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen readers that the navigation menu has closed. The open function correctly announces "Navigation menu opened", creating an inconsistent experience.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in the dropdownMenu module removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to report the menu as closed even when it is visible.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the faqAccordion module animates the panel and announces the expansion but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", leaving screen reader users unaware that the answer is expanded.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the faqAccordion module collapses the panel and updates ARIA states but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen readers that the answer has collapsed. The open function correctly announces "expanded", creating an inconsistent experience for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput missing aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: The _onApply() function validates the discount code and displays an error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input field when the code is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4100: In the else branch where `result.valid` is false, after setting `_statusEl.textContent`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function reveals the mobile navigation drawer which traps focus and blocks interaction with the rest of the page, functioning as a modal dialog, but it does not set aria-modal="true" on the drawer element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2296: Insert `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button lacks aria‑pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: Each dropdown trigger (`[data-dropdown] button`) toggles a submenu but never updates `aria-pressed`, violating the requirement for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: When opening the dropdown, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; when closing, set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger button lacks aria‑pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons toggle answer visibility but never modify `aria-pressed`, so the toggle state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', item.open ? 'true' : 'false')` whenever the item is opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: If the navigation drawer element does not have an `id`, the trigger button is left without an `aria-controls` attribute, breaking the required relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has a unique `id` (e.g., `id="nav-drawer"`), and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'nav-drawer')` in `init()`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger may lack aria‑controls when panel has no id
issuesFound.explanation: The registration code only adds `aria-controls` if the panel already has an `id`. If the panel is missing an `id`, the trigger ends up without `aria-controls`, breaking the required control‑panel relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Generate a unique `id` for the panel when none exists (e.g., `const panelId = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel');`) and always set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, and assistive technologies rely on this attribute to identify erroneous inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1387 (inside markInvalid function): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after the null check.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the menu, leaving users unaware that the panel is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1771 (inside open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The close function updates CSS classes and ARIA attributes to collapse the navigation drawer, but unlike the open() function, it does not call liveRegion.announce() to notify screen reader users that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1818 (inside close function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before the emit call or at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes to the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1958 (inside _openInstance function): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the dropdown panel but does not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users navigating with keyboard shortcuts (Enter/Space/ArrowDown) receive no confirmation that the dropdown has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1962 (inside _openInstance function): Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');` or similar announcement before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ panel by removing the hidden attribute and animating the height, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern and prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3428 (inside open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is closed (via Escape key or outside click), the function updates ARIA attributes and CSS classes but does not announce the closure to screen readers. Users may not realize the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1979 (inside _closeInstance function): Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator.validate does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The generic inline validator marks fields as invalid visually but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input element, preventing screen readers from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate()`, after a failed validation call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function opens the mobile navigation drawer but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open()` function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._fetchAndRender does not announce suggestion list updates via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After fetching suggestions, the code updates the suggestion list UI but never writes a message to a live region, so users of screen readers receive no feedback that results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering suggestions, call `liveRegion.announce(`${results.length} suggestion${results.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function calls `open()` or `close()` but does not ensure the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute reflects the new state when opening.
issuesFound.fix: After calling `open()`, set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (the `close()` already sets it to false).

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._updateStatus updates status text without aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The `_statusEl` element receives result‑count or error messages, but it lacks an `aria-live` attribute, so changes are not announced to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `_statusEl` has `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');` (or `assertive` for errors) when it is created.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened via `_openInstance()`, the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"`, breaking the accessibility relationship between trigger and panel.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_openInstance()`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` before showing the panel.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput._onApply updates status text without aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The discount code component writes feedback to `_statusEl` but does not set an `aria-live` attribute, so the result of applying a code is not announced to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onApply()`, after updating `_statusEl.textContent`, add `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'assertive');` (or `polite` for success).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and locks scroll but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state to open and removes hidden from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to false, causing screen readers to announce the wrong state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The paired _closeInstance sets it to false, leaving the expanded state incorrect for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent decision actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _onAccept and _onDecline functions store the decision and hide the banner but do not announce the outcome (e.g., "Cookie preferences saved") to screen readers via the live region. Users are left unaware if their action was successful.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator dynamic text updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _animate function updates the element's textContent repeatedly during the number animation. Without a live region, screen reader users may miss the final value or hear fragmented numbers. The aria-label is only set on completion.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator.validate() fails to ensure aria-invalid is set on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: This validation function calls srUtils.markInvalid() which omits aria-invalid, and does not supplement it. Consequently, form fields marked as invalid lack the aria-invalid attribute, preventing screen reader users from identifying validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3080 (inside validate function): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before or alongside the call to `srUtils.markInvalid(field, binding.errorEl)`.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard._validateCurrentStep() does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard validates fields using inlineValidator.validate() which fails to set aria-invalid. Since this is the entry point for wizard step validation, invalid fields in the wizard are not marked with aria-invalid, preventing screen reader users from identifying errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3718 (inside _validateCurrentStep function): After `const result = inlineValidator.validate(field);`, add a check `if (!result.valid) setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: contactForm._validateAll() does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: This validation function iterates through contact form fields and calls inlineValidator.validate(), which omits aria-invalid. Invalid fields are not programmatically marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3849 (inside _validateAll function): After `const result = inlineValidator.validate(field);`, add `if (!result.valid) setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm._onSubmit() does not set aria-invalid on invalid email field
issuesFound.explanation: When email validation fails, the code calls inlineValidator.validate() which does not set aria-invalid. The invalid email field lacks the aria-invalid attribute, preventing screen readers from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3944 (inside _onSubmit function): After `inlineValidator.validate(_emailEl);`, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() missing aria-pressed on toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons toggle between active and inactive states but the function only updates CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons, violating the toggle button pattern and leaving screen reader users unaware of the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2673 (inside forEach loop): Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The function declares a focus trap variable `_trap` but never activates it when opening the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile navigation drawer into the background content, violating the modal focus management pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1773 (inside open function): After `_open = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not return focus to trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, focus is not programmatically returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order and must navigate from the beginning of the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1819 (inside close function): Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` before the final emit or at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() does not return focus to trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is collapsed (closed), the function does not return focus to the trigger button that opened it. This violates the ARIA accordion pattern and disorients keyboard users who expect focus to remain on the control they activated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3472 (inside close function): Add `item.trigger.focus();` after setting the aria-expanded attribute to ensure focus returns to the button.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator.validate does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator removes visual error classes but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`, leaving the invalid state lingering for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In the success branch of `inlineValidator.validate()`, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (or set it to `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: siteSearch status element missing live region for “no results” and error messages
issuesFound.explanation: When there are zero suggestions or an error occurs, the status message is only visual; without `aria-live`, screen readers are unaware of the condition.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `_statusEl.textContent` for “no results” or error, also set `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'assertive');`.

issuesFound.explanation: Although the module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping and the close() function attempts to deactivate it, the open() function never initializes or activates the focus trap. This allows keyboard focus to escape the open mobile navigation drawer and move to background page content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not announce state change to screen readers

Users navigating with screen readers receive no confirmation that the menu has closed, leaving them uncertain of the UI state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2000: Add the following before the closing brace:
  `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger

This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is expanded. The paired _closeInstance() function correctly sets it to "false".

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not announce state change to screen readers

issuesFound.fix: Line 2128: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() does not announce state change to screen readers

issuesFound.fix:
Line 3470: Add the following before the closing brace:
  `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, _openInstance adds the CSS class but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the associated trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not announce the closing action via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close removes the drawer from view but does not send an announcement to the shared liveRegion, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback that the navigation has been closed.
issuesFound.fix: After the drawer is closed, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not announce the closing action via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is closed, the function updates CSS but never announces the state change, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu collapsed');` after the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function within dropdownMenu removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is now open, as the state attribute remains false.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function fails to activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The open function within mobileNav displays the navigation drawer but does not activate a focus trap (the _trap variable is declared but never initialized or activated). Keyboard focus can escape the modal drawer to the underlying page content, violating focus management requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); } _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close function within mobileNav hides the navigation drawer but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the liveRegion module. The open function announces "Navigation menu opened", but closing is silent, leaving users unsure if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function within faqAccordion removes the hidden attribute from the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating a state mismatch that confuses assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2775: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.fix: Line 2805: if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner show function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _showBanner function within cookieConsent injects and displays the consent dialog but does not announce its appearance to screen readers. Users may be unaware the dialog has appeared if focus is not immediately moved or if they are not monitoring the screen.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3735: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieBannerShown || 'Cookie consent banner displayed. Please review your preferences.');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item is expanded, the function animates the panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but it never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The close() function correctly sets it to "false", so the state is only accurate when collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3425: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The helper function adds an error description via aria-describedby but omits the aria-invalid="true" attribute. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of form fields to users. The paired markValid() correctly removes aria-invalid, but the invalid state is never programmatically set.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1082: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle button lacks aria‑pressed state indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav‑toggle button acts as a binary control (open/close) but never receives an aria‑pressed attribute, violating the requirement for toggle buttons to expose their pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle (or after each open/close), call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open));`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce the discount message via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the script updates the “Save %” badge but never announces the new discount to screen‑reader users, leaving them unaware of the benefit.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the savings message, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.savingsMessage || 'Discount applied');` or a custom message reflecting the new period.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator validation functions missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, the inlineValidator marks fields as invalid by adding error messages and CSS classes, but it never sets the aria-invalid attribute to "true" on invalid fields or clears it on valid fields. Assistive technologies cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`. Line srUtils.markValid: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` after removing the describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close instance functions show and hide the panel using the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they do not toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates, the count badge changes, and the empty state may appear, but no aria-live announcement is made to inform screen reader users of the result count or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found for current filter`);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions animate the panel visibility but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware of whether the answer panel is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`. Line faqAccordion.close: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the prices update visually with animation, but the change in billing period and prices is not announced to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: After updating the plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Prices updated to ${period} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Since the drawer is closed by default, the trigger must programmatically declare this state so screen readers know the initial state before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: After `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel toggle not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ panels are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced via the live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the content has changed (unless they manually check).
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : 'Answer expanded');`. Line faqAccordion.close: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : 'Answer collapsed');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on newsletter email field
issuesFound.explanation: `newsletterForm.init` binds the email input with `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email']);`. The inline validator shows visual error messages but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input when the value is invalid, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix: Extend `inlineValidator.validate` (or the validator’s `validateField` callback) to call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')` on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` in **dropdownMenu** add/remove CSS classes to show/hide the panel but never set or clear the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance` add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge(count)` updates the text of the badge but does not write to any element with `role="status"`/`aria-live`. Users of assistive technology are not informed when the number of matching products changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `
`) and set its text to “X products found” each time the count changes.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle does not announce the change
issuesFound.explanation: `_onViewToggle` switches the grid between grid and list view but does not provide an ARIA announcement, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the layout change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce('Product view changed to list view')` or similar, using the new mode’s label.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter sorting change is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `_onSortChange` updates the sort order and re‑applies filters, but no live‑region update informs users that “Sorted by price, ascending” (or whatever) has occurred.
issuesFound.fix: After applying the new sort, invoke `liveRegion.announce('Results sorted by price, ascending')`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in the srUtils module links an error message element to the field via aria-describedby but fails to set the aria-invalid attribute to "true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the mobileNav module toggles the navigation drawer visibility by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute from the overlay, but it fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module hides the navigation drawer by removing CSS classes and setting the hidden attribute on the overlay, but it fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button to "false". Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init function does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The init function in the mobileNav module locates the _trigger element but does not set an initial aria-expanded attribute on it. If the menu is closed on load (default), the trigger should have aria-expanded="false" to indicate its state to assistive technologies before any interaction occurs.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the faqAccordion module expands the answer panel by removing the hidden attribute and animating height, but it fails to set the aria-expanded attribute on the item.trigger button to "true". The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in the dropdownMenu module reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the inst.trigger button to "true". Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is open.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _updateCountBadge function does not announce count change
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function in the productFilter module updates the text content of the product count badge when filters are applied, but the badge element is not configured as a live region and no announcement is made. Screen reader users will not be informed of the new result count.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element's ID. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying which element is controlled by the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: After ensuring IDs for _trigger and _drawer (using srUtils.ensureId), add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, there is no announcement via the live region to confirm that the menu has closed, which can leave screen reader users uncertain about the current page state.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not programmatically moved to the drawer or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button while the visual context changes, making navigation difficult.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: After `_lockScroll();`, add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` and activate the focus trap if implemented.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. The focus is lost to the document body, forcing keyboard users to navigate back to the top of the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close: Before the emit call, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`. Ensure the _trap variable is properly initialized and deactivated to manage focus return.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on contact‑form “Full name” field
issuesFound.explanation: In `contactForm.init` each field is bound with `inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules);`. The validator never updates `aria-invalid`, so screen‑reader users receive no programmatic error indication.
issuesFound.fix: Modify the inline validator to set `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')` when validation fails and remove the attribute when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on contact‑form “Email address” field
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 3 for the email input; missing `aria-invalid` on validation failure.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 3.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on contact‑form “Message” field
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea is validated but never receives `aria-invalid="true"` on error, preventing AT feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 3.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter pagination change is not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `goToPage(page)` updates the displayed page of products but does not announce the new page number.
issuesFound.fix: After changing pages, call `liveRegion.announce('Page 2 of 5')` (or the appropriate numbers).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter text‑search input changes are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `_onTextFilterInput` debounces the search query and re‑filters products, but there is no live‑region feedback such as “3 products match your search”.
issuesFound.fix: After the debounce fires and filters are applied, update a hidden live‑region with the result count (e.g., `liveRegion.announce('3 products match your search')`).

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list is shown without an `aria-live` announcement of the result count
issuesFound.explanation: After fetching suggestions, `suggestionListRenderer.render` populates the list but does not announce how many suggestions are available. Users of assistive technology may not know that suggestions have appeared.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} suggestions available`)` where `count` is the number of items rendered.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function in the dropdownMenu module hides the dropdown panel by adding the hidden attribute and removing CSS classes, but it does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the inst.trigger button to "false". Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is closed.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid function does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function in the srUtils module removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but fails to set the aria-invalid attribute to "false" or remove it from the field. The field may remain in an invalid state in the accessibility tree even after correction.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod function does not announce price change
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function in the pricing module updates the text content of price display elements when the billing period changes, but it does not announce this change to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology will not know the prices have updated.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils adds an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the markInvalid function, after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the state change. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
