issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product cards and count badge but does not announce the filter results to screen readers. Users receive no feedback that the product list has changed or how many items match the current filters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5050: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${totalMatches} products match your filters\`);` (or use STRINGS.filterApplied if available).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide the dropdown panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. This leaves screen reader users unaware of the panel's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3160: In _openInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`.
  Line 3180: In _closeInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function updates price displays and billing labels when switching between monthly and annual billing, but does not announce these changes to screen readers. Users navigating with a screen reader are not informed that prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5650: After `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function announces when the navigation opens (via open()), but does not announce when it closes. Screen reader users are not informed that the menu has been dismissed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2980: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` inside the close() function, before or alongside the emit call.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu controller toggles the visibility of a submenu panel but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded state. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not set aria‑expanded to true when a panel opens
issuesFound.explanation: In faqAccordion.open() the panel is shown and animated, but the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never set to "true". Assistive technologies therefore think the item remains collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class but lacks aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() show and hide the drawer by adding/removing a CSS class, but they never add or remove aria‑hidden on the drawer element. When the drawer is off‑screen it remains in the accessibility tree, causing screen‑reader confusion.

issuesFound.title: Form inputs missing aria-invalid attribute during validation
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid and srUtils.markValid functions manage error descriptions but do not set or clear the aria-invalid attribute on the input field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid="true" to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3375: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');
  Line 3390: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the navigation drawer but does not announce the state change to screen readers. While open() calls liveRegion.announce(), close() does not, leaving users unaware the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1655: if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not trigger a live region announcement. Users are not informed when a submenu appears or disappears, which is critical for non-visual navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1835: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpen || 'Menu opened');
  Line 1850: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClose || 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu toggle the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Users relying on assistive technology will not hear the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1835: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1850: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items does not announce the change to screen readers. While search filtering announces results, the core toggle interaction is silent, degrading the experience for users who rely on audio feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2365: if (announceToSR) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) || 'Answer expanded');
  Line 2395: if (announceToSR) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) || 'Answer collapsed');

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Accepting or declining cookie consent hides the banner but does not announce the result to screen readers. Users are left unsure if their preference was saved or if the banner was dismissed successfully.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3880: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');
  Line 3890: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid cleanup
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute from the field. The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2535: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` inside the markValid function, after updating aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing dialog semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog (trapping focus) but does not have role="dialog" or aria-modal="true" set on the drawer element when opened. Screen readers may not treat it as a modal and allow navigation outside the drawer.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2960: In the open() function, after `_open = true;`, add:
  `if (_drawer) { _drawer.setAttribute('role', 'dialog'); setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.MODAL, 'true'); }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer element lacks an accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby) when opened as a dialog. Screen readers announce it only as a generic "dialog" or by its landmark role without identifying its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2960: In the open() function, alongside setting role and aria-modal, add:
  `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.LABEL, 'Mobile navigation menu');` (or reference a heading ID if available via aria-labelledby).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers may report the item as collapsed even when open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2365: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: Product count badge missing aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function in productFilter updates the text content of the count badge but the element lacks an aria-live attribute. Screen reader users are not notified when the number of visible products changes after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2135: setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates the displayed prices and toggle state but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing"). Users may not realize the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2635: liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function not updating aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open action not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function finds the trigger button (_trigger) but does not initialise the aria-expanded attribute to "false" on page load, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial state.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField zone missing aria-describedby for errors
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField.bind function creates a status element but does not link it to the upload zone via aria-describedby, and error messages are only announced via live region but not associated with the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4448: Add setAttr(zoneEl, ARIA.DESCRIBEDBY, statusEl.id); after creating the status element.

issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: Add removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); or setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); after clearing the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input lacks aria-invalid on error

issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator._renderResult` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` when `!result.valid` and remove it when valid.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles each dropdown menu (e.g. `button[data-dropdown-trigger]`) changes the panel’s visibility but never updates its `aria-expanded` attribute. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', true)` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', false)` when closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the product count badge (`[data-product-count]`) is updated with plain text, but there is no `aria-live` region announcing the new count. Users of assistive technology are unaware that the number of results changed.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden live region (e.g., ``) and update its text with the new count each time the filter is applied.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search status element not marked as live region
issuesFound.explanation: The status element (`_statusEl`) used to convey loading, error, or “no results” messages is created without an `aria-live` attribute, so updates are not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: When creating `_statusEl`, set `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite')` (or `assertive` for errors) and ensure it remains hidden when empty.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slide change not announced with role="region"
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel updates the visible slide but does not set `role="region"` and `aria-live` on the slide container, so AT users may miss the change.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(slide, 'role', 'region')` and `setAttr(slide, 'aria-live', 'polite')` to each slide element, and update its `aria-label` with the slide number on each transition.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid()` helper function adds the error element to `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. Form validation errors will not be exposed to assistive technologies as invalid states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1383: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer using CSS classes but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1726: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1753: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod()` function updates visible price values across all plan cards when the billing period changes but does not announce this change to screen readers via an `aria-live` region. Users may not realize prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2486: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module manage the panel's `hidden` attribute and CSS classes but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `inst.trigger` button. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1923: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1936: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters()` function updates the visible product grid and count badge based on user selection but does not announce the new result count or filter state to screen readers via an `aria-live` region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2246: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} shown`);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `faqAccordion` sets the panel to visible and updates CSS classes but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `item.trigger`. The `close()` function correctly sets it to false, creating an asymmetric state management failure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2766: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not remove aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid()` helper function removes the error description reference but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute on the field. Fields may remain marked as invalid to screen readers even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1396: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); // ... existing code ...

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init function missing aria-controls and initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `init()` function for `mobileNav` identifies the `_trigger` and `_drawer` but does not ensure `aria-controls` is set on the trigger pointing to the drawer's ID, nor does it set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1806: const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs not updating aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function updates the visual active state of filter tab buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute (or aria-selected for tab pattern) to indicate which filter is currently active to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2918: Add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop to reflect the active state.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price updates visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region, so users may not be aware the prices have switched.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3260: Add liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly); after updating the prices.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs aria-pressed not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.init function sets up filter tabs but does not initialise the aria-pressed attribute (or aria-selected) on the tab buttons to reflect the initial active state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2995: Add _syncTabAriaStates(); call should include aria-pressed updates (see Issue 4 fix), and ensure initial state is set by calling the updated sync function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable to hold a focus trap instance, but never initialises it with createFocusTrap. When the menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab out of the modal navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1968: Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); after _initialised = true;
  Line 1897: Change if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.activate(); to if (_trap) _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.init function sets up the play/pause button (_playBtn) but does not initialise the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the initial auto-play state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4074: Add setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_playing)); after checking if _playBtn exists.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function finds the clear button (_clearBtn) but does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label) if the HTML element lacks text content or an aria-label attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2675: Add if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute(ARIA.LABEL) && !_clearBtn.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(_clearBtn, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search'); }

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher toggle buttons not initialising aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.init function sets up event listeners but does not initialise the aria-pressed state on the toggle buttons to reflect the current theme preference on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4635: Add set(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current)); inside the forEach loop for theme toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” input missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The “Full name” field (`[name="full_name"]`) is validated via `inlineValidator.bind`, yet the validation does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on failure, so errors are not conveyed to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the validation result adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input when invalid and removes it when corrected.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” input missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The email field (`[name="email"]`) is validated but never receives `aria-invalid="true"` on error, preventing screen‑readers from announcing the problem.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on validation failure and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” input missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The subject field (`[name="subject"]`) is validated but the script does not set `aria-invalid` when the value is invalid, so the error is invisible to ATs.
issuesFound.fix: Apply `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on error and remove it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea (`[name="message"]`) is validated for length, but the validation does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on failure, leaving the error undisclosed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation result handling, set `aria-invalid="true"` when the textarea is invalid and clear it when valid.

issuesFound.title: Contact form consent checkbox missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The consent checkbox (`[name="consent"]`) is required, but when unchecked the validation does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, so AT users are not informed of the missing required state.
issuesFound.fix: When the checkbox fails validation, add `aria-invalid="true"`; remove it once the user checks the box.

issuesFound.title: suggestionListRenderer mount function missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mount()` function initializes the search combobox attributes (`role`, `aria-controls`, `aria-haspopup`) but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on the input element. The expanded state is only set when the list opens or closes dynamically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1966: setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function marks fields as invalid using CSS classes and aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid form fields. Screen readers will not convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4228: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` inside the else branch (when result.valid is false).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module toggles the navigation drawer visibility by adding CSS classes and emitting events, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1315: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not set aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: When opening the search suggestions panel, this function sets _searchState.open to true but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (_inputEl), which has role="combobox". Screen readers will not announce that the suggestion list is displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2852: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers believing the menu is still expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: This function filters products, updates counts, and shows/hide cards, but does not write to an aria-live region to announce the filter results or count changes to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);` after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When switching between monthly and annual billing, this function updates prices and UI state but does not announce the change to screen readers via the liveRegion utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3725: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after updating the prices.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, this function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1423: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick does not announce action to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When activated via click, this function scrolls to top and moves focus, but does not announce the action to screen readers. Focus movement alone is not sufficient for users who may not realize the page position changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` before or after the scroll operation.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the _renderResult function removes error classes and describedby references, but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4222: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` inside the if (result.valid) branch.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not set aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the search suggestions, this function sets _searchState.open to false but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the input element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2864: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not announce navigation opened
issuesFound.explanation: The function emits a custom event but does not announce via the liveRegion that the navigation menu has opened, leaving screen reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1323: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` after opening the drawer.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: Validation errors are not exposed to AT because aria‑invalid is missing.
issuesFound.fix:
In the error branch add:  
```js
setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
```

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator number change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator _animate() function updates the number textContent dynamically but does not announce the final value or the change via a live region. Users are not informed when the counter completes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5365: liveRegion.announce(`Statistic updated to ${_format(cfg.target, cfg)}`);

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter column highlight not conveyed to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The planComparisonHighlighter highlightColumn() function toggles a CSS class to highlight a plan column but does not update aria-current or aria-selected. Screen reader users are not informed which plan is highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3935: setAttr(col, ARIA.CURRENT, String(isTarget));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds an error message element to the field's aria-describedby but fails to set the aria-invalid attribute to "true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1783: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); inside the markInvalid function after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled via the open() and close() functions, which add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the panel. However, neither function calls setAttribute('aria-expanded', ...) or assigns to .ariaExpanded on the _trigger element (the hamburger button). Screen readers cannot announce whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2327: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', true); inside the open function after setting _open = true. Line 2362: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', false); inside the close function after setting _open = false.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close actions do not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer opens and closes in response to user actions (click, keydown, outside click), but neither the open nor close function writes to an aria-live region to announce the change to screen reader users. The announceToSR parameter exists but is never used to trigger an announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2327: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened'); inside open(). Line 2362: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); inside close().

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, which manipulate the hidden attribute and CSS classes. Neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open or closed state, leaving screen reader users unaware of the panel visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', true); inside _openInstance. Line 2495: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', false); inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions do not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a user opens or closes a dropdown menu, the visible change is not announced to screen readers. The functions emit custom events but do not write to an aria-live region (role="status" or aria-live="polite").
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened'); inside _openInstance. Line 2495: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed'); inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually and the toggle state changes, but the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") is not announced via the live region. Screen reader users may not notice that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4830 (after setPeriod call in _onToggleClick or inside setPeriod itself): Add liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it locates the trigger button (_trigger) and drawer (_drawer) but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger to indicate that the closed drawer is initially collapsed. This violates the requirement that ARIA state attributes reflect the current state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2427 (after _initialised = true): Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', false);. Also ensure setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id); is added if _drawer has an id.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce filter results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user applies a filter to the product grid, the visible cards change and the count badge updates, but the result count and filter change are not announced to screen readers. The function emits a custom event but does not write to an aria-live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4427 (inside filterAnimation.run callback): Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. Invalid fields that become valid retain aria-invalid="true", misleading screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1793: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); or removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid'); inside the markValid function after updating aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on combobox input at page load
issuesFound.explanation: During initialisation, the search input is configured with role="combobox" and aria-controls referencing the listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" to indicate that the suggestion listbox is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4080 (after const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);): Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', false);.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: Validation adds an error message element but never toggles the field’s `aria-invalid` attribute, so screen readers cannot perceive that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
When `result.valid` is false, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and when valid, remove or set to `'false'`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` (and the `open()` / `close()` helpers) add or remove CSS classes to show or hide the navigation drawer, but they never set or update the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the drawer state, add  
  ```js
  setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open));
  ```  
  (or `String(_open)` after opening) in both `open()` and `close()`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panel hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The answer panel is hidden with `hidden` but never receives `aria-hidden`, so it may still be announced.
issuesFound.fix:
After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')`, add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Remove when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure button toggles a panel’s visibility via class changes, yet the button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never changed, leaving the state invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `_onTriggerClick` (or the `_openInstance` / `_closeInstance` helpers) add:  
  ```js
  setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!inst.open));
  ```

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the navigation drawer does not provide an audible cue, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After the drawer state changes, call  
  ```js
  liveRegion.announce(_open ? STRINGS.navOpen : STRINGS.navClosed);
  ```

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation button acts as a binary switch (open / closed) but never reflects its state with `aria‑pressed`.
issuesFound.fix:
After each toggle, update the attribute, e.g.  
  ```js
  setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(!_open));
  ```

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (`data-billing-toggle`) behaves like a binary switch but never reflects its state with `aria-pressed`, breaking the required ARIA pattern for switches.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, add:  
  ```js
  setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));
  ```

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion panel does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input shows/hides the suggestion list, but the input never receives an `aria-expanded` update, so screen readers cannot detect the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_open()` and `_close()` add:  
  ```js
  setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', String(_searchState.open));
  ```

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: No live‑region announcement when a dropdown panel is shown or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, add  
  ```js
  liveRegion.announce(inst.open ? STRINGS.dropdownOpened : STRINGS.dropdownClosed);
  ```

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) controls the drawer (`#nav-menu`) but no `aria-controls` attribute is set, breaking the explicit relationship required for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()` (or after the drawer element is cached) add  
  ```js
  setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));
  ```

---

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markInvalid() function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1450: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1705: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1725: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions do not announce the state change (e.g., "Navigation menu opened") to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT are not informed of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1705: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
  Line 1725: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). The state is undefined until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions do not announce the dropdown state change to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1910: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');
  Line 1930: liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips _onChipClick() function removes a filter chip but does not announce the filter removal (e.g., "Category filter removed") to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${label} removed`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing setPeriod() function updates the visible prices but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300: liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions toggle the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1910: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1930: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() and close() functions do not announce the FAQ answer expansion or collapse to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:

  Line 2770: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) || 'Answer collapsed');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markValid() function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute on the field. The invalid state may persist for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1470: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown menu pair, the code sets the panel to hidden but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The trigger lacks the expanded state attribute until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2590 (after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', false);.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick does not announce scroll action via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user clicks the scroll-to-top button, the page scrolls to the top but the action is not announced to screen readers. The keyboard shortcut handler (navKeyboardShortcuts) does announce this, but the click handler does not.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2671: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page'); inside _onClick.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not return focus to trigger when closing
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function accepts a returnFocus parameter but never uses it to programmatically return focus to the trigger button. When the dropdown closes (e.g., via Escape key or outside click), focus is lost to the document body instead of returning to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2495: Add if (returnFocus && inst.trigger) inst.trigger.focus(); at the end of the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _onDocumentClick does not return focus to input when closing suggestions
issuesFound.explanation: When the user clicks outside the search component, the suggestion panel closes via _close(), but focus is not returned to the search input element. Keyboard focus is lost, forcing the user to tab back to the search field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4042: Change _close(); to _close(); if (_inputEl) _inputEl.focus();.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is parsed, the trigger button receives no `aria-expanded` attribute, leaving its default state undefined.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `trigger`, add:  
  ```js
  setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
  ```

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The switch controls the pricing cards but does not reference them via `aria-controls`, so assistive tech cannot associate the control with the region it affects.
issuesFound.fix:
After locating `_toggleEl` and the element that receives the price change, add:  
  ```js
  setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'pricing-grid'));
  ```

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the drawer only removes CSS classes; the drawer remains in the accessibility tree because `aria-hidden` is never applied.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()`, after hiding the drawer, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `open()` remove it.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute but lack `aria-hidden`, which can keep them perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `_closeInstance`, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')`, also add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Remove the attribute when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The listbox is hidden via the `hidden` attribute only; `aria-hidden` is not updated, leaving the list potentially exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `hide()`, after `_listEl.setAttribute('hidden', '')`, also call `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In `show()`, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ item open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Users receive no spoken feedback when an FAQ answer expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing state, call  
  ```js
  liveRegion.announce(inst.open ? `${item.question} — answer expanded` : `${item.question} — answer collapsed`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard step change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Advancing to the next step updates the UI but does not announce the new step, making it invisible to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
After `goToStep`, add  
  ```js
  liveRegion.announce(`Step ${_current+1} of ${_steps.length}`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer (duplicate of 12, but kept for clarity)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer remains in the accessibility tree after being hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` on close and remove on open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register() function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger element. The state is undefined on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1980: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function sets the panel to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close() function correctly sets it to false, causing state desynchronization.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter _updateCountBadge() function updates the visible product count but does not announce the change (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count) || `${count} products showing`);

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner display not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent _showBanner() function displays the consent dialog but does not announce its appearance to screen readers via a live region. Users may not know the banner has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: liveRegion.announceAssertive('Cookie consent banner displayed. Please accept or decline.');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator _renderResult does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the function updates the visual state and associates an error message via aria-describedby, but it fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: In the invalid branch (else block), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after the markInvalid call. In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown and hidden using the hidden attribute, but the trigger button (inst.trigger) does not have its aria-expanded attribute synchronized to reflect the visibility state. Assistive technologies cannot announce whether the dropdown menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 3135 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the start of the function before removing the open class.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count is updated in the DOM via _updateCountBadge, but this change is not announced to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies are not informed that the filter results have changed or how many items are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: After the line `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open/_close do not update aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox is shown and hidden dynamically, but the combobox input element (_inputEl) does not have its aria-expanded attribute updated to reflect whether the popup is visible. Screen readers cannot inform users when the suggestion list appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3840 (in _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line 3855 (in _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually via _updatePlanCard, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: After the forEach loop that calls `_updatePlanCard(plan, period)`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes with the menu in the closed state, the trigger button (_trigger) does not have aria-expanded="false" set, violating the requirement that ARIA states must reflect the initial state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: After the line `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);` and the null check, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but fails to initialize aria-expanded="false" for the closed state on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: After the line `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opening is a significant UI change, but it is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the navigation menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: After the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription form validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: In newsletterForm._onSubmit() the email field is validated, but when the value is invalid no aria‑invalid attribute is applied, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: After the validation check, call setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true') on failure and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobile navigation module change the drawer’s visibility but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') in open() and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') in close().

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons are not given an aria‑expanded attribute in the initial DOM, so their collapsed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.init() or during faqRegistry.parse(), set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') for each item.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button ( .nav‑toggle ) is never given an aria‑expanded attribute on page load, so its default state is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') after the element is cached.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._updateCountBadge() updates the numeric badge but does not write to an aria‑live region, so users of assistive technology are unaware of result‑count changes.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> element (e.g., #product-count‑status) and set its textContent to the new count inside _updateCountBadge().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button toggles the menu but never reflects its pressed state via aria‑pressed, violating the ARIA pattern for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle() or after each open()/close() call, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', _open ? 'true' : 'false').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() adds the open class to the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated button, so screen readers cannot detect the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and on close set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() emit custom events but do not update any aria‑live region, leaving screen‑reader users uninformed when an answer expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix: Create a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> (e.g., #faq-status) and set its textContent to “Answer expanded” or “Answer collapsed” inside the respective functions.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: open() and close() change the menu visibility but do not provide an audible cue via an aria‑live region, so screen‑reader users are not notified of the navigation state change.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> (e.g., #nav-status) and set its textContent to “Navigation menu opened” or “Navigation menu closed” inside open() and close().

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks a field as invalid visually and adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1655 (markInvalid): field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle functions change the visible state of the drawer and overlay but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2305 (open): setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
Line ~2330 (close): setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced via aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge text is updated dynamically when filters change, but the element is not configured as a live region. Screen reader users are not announced the new count.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3110 (_updateCountBadge): setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu toggle functions show and hide the panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2510 (_openInstance): setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
Line ~2530 (_closeInstance): setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle does not update aria-checked or aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle updates visual classes but does not update aria-checked (for role="switch") or aria-pressed (for buttons). The selected state is not available to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~3440 (_syncToggleState): setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));
Line ~3445 (_syncToggleState): setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown instance is registered, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not initialised to "false" if it is missing from the HTML. The initial state is ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2595 (end of register): if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: This utility function is responsible for marking form fields as invalid but only manages the aria-describedby relationship, omitting the required aria-invalid="true" attribute that screen readers rely on to identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` at the end of the markInvalid function after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, this function removes the error description reference but does not reset the aria-invalid attribute to "false" or remove it, potentially leaving stale invalid state information on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after clearing the describedby references in the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ panels expand with animation when opened, but this state change and the new content availability are not announced to screen readers. Users may not be aware that additional content is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: After the line `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ panels collapse when closed, but this state change is not announced to screen readers, potentially leaving users unaware that content has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After the line `removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer closing is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the menu has been dismissed, especially if focus is not explicitly managed.
issuesFound.fix: After the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm _onSubmit does not set aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When email validation fails, the function calls inlineValidator.validate and focuses the field, but does not ensure aria-invalid="true" is set on the email input to communicate the invalid state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: After the line `inlineValidator.validate(_emailEl);` and before focusing, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. On successful validation, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that control dropdown panels are not given an aria‑expanded attribute in the initial markup, leaving their default closed state undisclosed.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init() or when registering a trigger, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: open(item) animates the answer panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the question button, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') inside open(item).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The period‑selection buttons (data‑period) toggle pricing but never reflect their state with aria‑expanded, preventing screen‑reader users from knowing which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod() or _syncToggleState(), add setAttr(btn, 'aria-expanded', btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period ? 'true' : 'false').

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons lack an aria‑expanded attribute on load, so the default period is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: After pricing.init(), set setAttr(btn, 'aria-expanded', btn.getAttribute('data-period') === _pricingState.period ? 'true' : 'false') for each button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices but does not inform screen‑reader users via an aria‑live region, violating dynamic‑content announcement requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> (e.g., #pricing-status) and update its textContent to “Billing period switched to Annual” (or appropriate label) inside pricing.setPeriod().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function expands the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, while the close function correctly sets it to "false". State is inconsistent.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3690 (open): setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search component opens and closes the suggestion listbox but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input. Screen reader users are not informed of the list visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~3035 (_open): setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
Line ~3045 (_close): setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not set initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input's aria-expanded attribute is not initialised to "false" on page load. The initial state of the suggestion list is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3150 (end of init): if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons indicate the active filter via styling but do not programmatically communicate their pressed state to assistive technology. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1054: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when in error state, but the FormGroup component is not provided with errorId="newsletter-email-error", so the error message element never receives this ID in the DOM. This creates a broken reference that prevents screen readers from announcing the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1475: Change <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}> to <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Hero video play/pause button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles background video playback uses only aria‑label to convey state. Without aria‑pressed, AT cannot convey the toggle nature of the control.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button uses incorrect loading prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component expects an isLoading prop to set aria‑busy, but the code passes loading, so the loading state is not announced to AT.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the navigation dropdown does not expose its open/closed state to assistive technology because it lacks an aria‑expanded attribute. Keyboard and screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ item button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles each FAQ panel does not indicate its expanded state via aria‑expanded, leaving AT users unaware of whether the answer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual buttons act as a toggle group but do not expose their pressed state, so users cannot tell which period is active.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons indicate selection only via visual styling; they lack aria‑pressed, so AT cannot convey which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input references missing error element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-describedby points to newsletter-email-error, but no element with that id exists, breaking the reference.
issuesFound.fix:
After the <TextInput> for the email, add:  
Line 940:    <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input missing associated error element
issuesFound.explanation: aria-describedby references contact-name-error which is not rendered, leaving the error message inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix:
After the <TextInput> for name, add:  
Line 1004:    <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input missing associated error element
issuesFound.explanation: aria-describedby references contact-email-error which does not exist.
issuesFound.fix:
After the <TextInput> for email, add:  
Line 1024:    <ErrorMessage id="contact-email-error" message={email.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input missing associated error element
issuesFound.explanation: aria-describedby references contact-subject-error which is absent.
issuesFound.fix:
After the <TextInput> for subject, add:  
Line 1044:    <ErrorMessage id="contact-subject-error" message={subject.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: TextInput component fails to expose invalid state to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component receives an `invalid` prop for styling error states but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen readers cannot announce when the field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~941: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the input element props.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button controls a disclosure panel via aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the answer content is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2263: Add aria-expanded={isOpen ? 'true' : 'false'} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual billing toggle buttons indicate the selected state visually via CSS classes but do not expose this state programmatically via aria-pressed, preventing assistive technology users from knowing which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1976: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly' ? 'true' : 'false'} to the Monthly button
  Line ~1984: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual' ? 'true' : 'false'} to the Annual button

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input references non-existent error message ID
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup component does not receive the errorId prop, so the error message element is never rendered with that ID, creating a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2382: Add errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error} to the FormGroup wrapping the newsletter email field.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑invalid (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email <TextInput> receives an “invalid” prop but does not render aria‑invalid, so AT is not informed of validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Disclosure trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> that opens the navigation dropdown (NavDropdown component) does not have an aria‑expanded attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Clickable backdrop missing role/button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation backdrop is a <div> with an onClick handler but no role or keyboard support, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 3 for the second filter button.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑invalid (contact name)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Full name” TextInput sets invalid prop but does not output aria‑invalid, leaving AT unaware of the error state.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure trigger button in NavDropdown controls the visibility of the submenu (via isOpen prop) but does not communicate this state to assistive technology. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 530: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div missing accessible role and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div is interactive (onClick={onClose}) but lacks a role, tabIndex, and keyboard handler. It also incorrectly uses aria-modal="true" (valid only on role="dialog"). It should be aria-hidden to avoid cluttering the accessibility tree, as Escape key closes the modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: <div className="modal-overlay" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The video play/pause button is a two-state toggle (isPlaying) that changes aria-label text but does not use aria-pressed. Assistive technology relies on aria-pressed to identify toggle buttons reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 835: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs reference non-existent error message IDs via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput elements reference error message IDs (contact-name-error, contact-email-error, etc.) via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup wrapper never renders these IDs because the errorId prop is not passed. This creates broken ARIA references that point to elements not in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2500: Add errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.error} to the FormGroup for the name field
  Line ~2515: Add errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error} to the FormGroup for the email field
  Line ~2530: Add errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.error} to the FormGroup for the subject field
  Line ~2545: Add errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.error} to the FormGroup for the message field

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The “Monthly” button in BillingToggle does not expose its pressed state via aria‑pressed, so AT cannot tell which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑invalid (contact email)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Email address” TextInput in the contact form does not render aria‑invalid when invalid.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑invalid (contact subject)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Subject” TextInput does not expose its error state via aria‑invalid.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑invalid (contact message)
issuesFound.explanation: The message <Textarea> does not render aria‑invalid when the character limit is exceeded or the field is empty.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑describedby (newsletter email error)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input references “newsletter-email-error” via aria-describedby, but no element with that id is ever rendered, so the error message is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an error element with the matching id inside the FormGroup, e.g.:
  Line 958: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (newsletter submit)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form’s submit <Button> receives a “loading” prop (not the component’s isLoading prop), so aria‑busy is never set and AT cannot know the button is busy.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button in FaqItemComponent controls the visibility of the answer panel (via isOpen prop) but does not communicate this state to assistive technology. Screen reader users will not know if the answer is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1275: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons in the FilterTabs component act as toggle buttons where one is active at a time (controlled by activeFilter state), but they do not communicate their pressed state to assistive technology. Without aria-pressed, screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria‑describedby points to non‑existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: `aria-describedby` references `newsletter-email-error`, but no element with that ID is ever rendered, so AT cannot locate the error message.
issuesFound.fix:
Either render an element with that ID or remove the reference. Example fix (remove reference):  
  `Line 880: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} />`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs button missing aria‑pressed (All tabs)
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons convey selection only via visual styling; without aria‑pressed AT cannot know which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix:
For each button inside `FILTER_TABS.map` (e.g., line 566):  
  `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}`  
  Example corrected line:  
  `Line 566: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle “Monthly” button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle does not expose its pressed state, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 724: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle “Annual” button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 11 for the annual option.
issuesFound.fix: Line 734: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The email input in NewsletterForm has an aria-describedby attribute pointing to 'newsletter-email-error', but the corresponding ErrorMessage element is never rendered because the parent FormGroup receives an 'error' prop instead of the required 'errorId' and 'errorMessage' props. The referenced ID does not exist in the DOM.

Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently selected. This affects all 5 filter tabs ('all', 'scanning', 'design', 'testing', 'reporting').

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid prop
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states but does not map this to aria-invalid. Screen reader users cannot perceive when the input contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle button missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>
