issuesFound.fix: Line 576: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button acts as a radio/switch but lacks aria-pressed. Assistive technology users are not informed whether this billing option is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1158: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button acts as a radio/switch but lacks aria-pressed. Assistive technology users are not informed whether this billing option is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1165: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk indicating a required field is rendered as text without aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce the asterisk character literally (e.g., "star"), which is confusing and redundant since the field's required state is already conveyed programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 598: *

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection uses a <section> element without aria-labelledby or aria-label. Although it contains an h1, the section landmark itself is not named, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify this region during landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 867: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in DesktopNav apply a visual 'current' class but do not include aria-current="page". Assistive technology users are not informed which link represents the current page, reducing orientation within the site structure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 669: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in MobileNav apply a visual 'current' class but do not include aria-current="page". Assistive technology users are not informed which link represents the current page, reducing orientation within the site structure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 739: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section lacks aria-labelledby referencing its internal heading (id="stats-heading"). This omits an accessible name for the landmark, hindering screen reader users who navigate by region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 957: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: Button component root missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not pass aria-busy to the underlying button element. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is in a busy/loading state, which degrades the experience during form submission or data fetching.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel's close button contains only a visual "✕" symbol with no aria-label. Screen reader users hear only "button" with no indication that it closes the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name input references aria-describedby="contact-name-error" but the FormGroup wrapper lacks errorId="contact-name-error", so the error message is not programmatically associated.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references aria-describedby="contact-email-error" but the FormGroup wrapper lacks errorId="contact-email-error", breaking the association with the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea references aria-describedby including "contact-message-error" but the FormGroup wrapper lacks errorId="contact-message-error", so the error message is not linked.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection component renders a <section> containing a heading with id="hero-heading", but the section lacks aria-labelledby. As a major page region, it should have an accessible name for landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1354: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div interactive without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div handles click events to close the dialog but is not focusable or keyboard operable. It lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard handlers, making it inaccessible to keyboard users who cannot press Escape.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: <div role="button" aria-label="Close dialog" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} className="modal-overlay" ...>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Testimonial slides that are not currently visible (isVisible === false) are not marked with aria-hidden. Screen readers will read content from all slides, including those not displayed, creating a confusing experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1940: <div ... aria-hidden={!isVisible} ...>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation:
The category filter buttons act as a toggle group but lack `aria-pressed`, so the selected filter is not announced.  
  Fix (example for one button; apply to all):  
  Line 492:    <button  
  Line 493:      type="button"  
  Line 494:      aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}   // ← add this prop  
  Line 495:      className={[…].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}  
  Line 496:      tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1}  
  Line 497:      onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: Stats bar section missing accessible name (aria‑labelledby)
issuesFound.explanation: The `<section>` containing the statistics does not reference its hidden heading (`h2` with id “stats-heading”), leaving the landmark unnamed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 236:    <section  
  Line 237:      className="stats-bar"  
  Line 238:      aria-labelledby="stats-heading"   // ← add this prop

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing aria‑labelledby
issuesFound.explanation:
Each pricing plan’s `<article>` lacks an `aria-labelledby` that points to its heading (`h3` with id `headingId`), so the card has no accessible name.  
  Fix (example for one card):  
  Line 618:    <article  
  Line 619:      className="pricing-card__article"  
  Line 620:      aria-labelledby={headingId}   // ← add this prop

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element wrapping skip links lacks an aria-label. While there is only one such nav in this component, distinguishing it helps screen reader navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article element missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The article element in PlanCard contains a heading but does not reference it via aria-labelledby. This prevents the card from having a distinct accessible name in a list of plans.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1305: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in the mobile menu that represent the current page apply a visual class but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed which link corresponds to the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 795: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div has aria-modal instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". This attribute should be on the dialog element to correctly indicate modality to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1575: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
